blocks.text: Subject: Progress meeting, held at this office at the request of {{fact:f:E-2007-001.client}}.

items: Pamela Warren, Project Engineer, Brackenridge Civil Group Inc
Jason Jordan, Design Technician, Brackenridge Civil Group Inc
Kimberly Branch, Design Technician, Brackenridge Civil Group Inc
Carol Lewis, Director of Operations, Peck-Jennings

blocks.text: Pamela Warren reviewed the condition survey against the record drawings. Delamination of the deck is confined to the two spans nearest the west abutment, and to the shoulder areas rather than the wheel paths. The joints at both abutments are failing and will be replaced. This is consistent with what was assumed when the work was scoped.

Carol Lewis asked whether the crossing can remain open during construction. Pamela Warren said that half-width construction under one-way alternating traffic is workable given the deck width, and that the plans will show it that way, with a note permitting the contractor to propose a full closure if the schedule benefit is real. Carol Lewis said a full closure of more than one working day would not be acceptable and asked that the plans say so. Pamela Warren agreed to add it to the traffic control notes.

Jason Jordan reported that the plan set is laid out, the sheet index is issued, and the sheets are drawn as far as the survey supports. He noted that two sheets of the record drawings furnished to us are illegible where they were faxed, and asked whether legible originals exist. Carol Lewis will check her files.

The preliminary submittal was discussed. It will be issued at sixty percent for review by {{fact:f:E-2007-001.client}} and by the agency having jurisdiction over the crossing. Comments will be answered in writing, in the order they are issued. Nothing raised in the meeting changed the scope of the work or the fee.

Kimberly Branch asked for the intended treatment at the joint header before the detail is drawn, so that it is not drawn twice. Pamela Warren will mark up a sketch. Kimberly Branch also asked which spelling of the client's name is to appear in the title block. Carol Lewis provided the correct form and it will be used on every sheet.

Drainage of the deck was discussed. Pamela Warren said the existing scuppers are undersized and partly blocked, and that the rehabilitation will replace them rather than clean them, since the deck is open in those areas regardless. Carol Lewis asked whether that adds cost. Pamela Warren said it is within the scope as written and does not.

Carol Lewis asked when she would see something. Jason Jordan said the preliminary set is the first thing worth looking at and that an incomplete set circulated early causes more questions than it answers. Carol Lewis accepted this and asked only that she be told before it is transmitted, so that she can arrange her own review. Pamela Warren agreed to telephone in advance.

rows: Add the traffic control note limiting a full closure to one working day.
Mark up the joint header treatment for drafting.
Locate legible originals of the two illegible record drawing sheets.
Plot a check set of the preliminary sheets for internal review.
Draw the joint header and approach grading details on receipt of the sketch.

blocks.text: The next progress meeting will be arranged by telephone. Corrections to these minutes should be given to Kimberly Branch within one week. Otherwise they stand as written and go in the project file.
